document.docTitle: Crostis boreas Prena & Anderson 2022, new species
document.masterDocTitle: The genus Crostis Casey (Coleoptera: Curculionidae: Baridinae) in North America

mods:titleInfo.mods:title: The genus Crostis Casey (Coleoptera: Curculionidae: Baridinae) in North America

mods:name.mods:affiliation: Beaty Center for Species Discovery, Canadian Museum of Nature, PO Box 3443, Station D, Ottawa, ON. K 1 P 6 P 4, Canada. randerson @ mus-nature. ca; https: // orcid. org / 0000 - 0003 - 0665 - 2977

subSubSection.paragraph:
Diagnosis. Total length 2.2–3.0 mm; body form oblong-ovate, L/W elytra 1.19–1.43 ( Figs. 1–6 ). Characteristic for this most northern species of
Crostis
are (i) transverse pronotum with disc longitudinally striate and posterior (only) portion of flanks striate or with elongate punctures ( Figs. 11–12 ), (ii) length of first funicular article subequal to combined lengths of articles 2–4, (iii) punctures of ventrites I and II smaller, lateralmost areas of ventrite I with 3–4 punctures along length ( Fig. 18 ), and (iv) penis with apex truncate, slightly expanded laterally towards base, endophallus distally with asymmetrical, elongate, fluted, gutter-like endophallite with one margin longer, much thicker and more heavily sclerotized than the other, duct attached to the latter, apodemes ca. 1.7× longer than length of penis body ( Fig. 20 ).

caption.paragraph:
FIGURES 1–6.
Crostis boreas
, dorsal and lateral aspects. 1, 2, elongate male, Texas; 3, elongate female, Querétaro; 4, moderately stout female, Querétaro; 5, 6, very stout male, Querétaro. Scale bars 0.5 mm.

subSubSection.paragraph: Etymology. The name is derived from Ancient Greek βορέας, referring to the North.

Natural history.
Most specimens have been sifted from leaf litter in various types of forests and one specimen was sifted from leaf litter in a cave. No host associations are known. In Mexico , specimens have been collected in forests from
1400–3100 m
.

Material examined.
Holotype male,
USA : Texas ,
Brewster County , Big Bend National Park , Oak Canyon ,
1463 m
,
8 Sep 1988
, R . Anderson, berlese hardwood litter ( CMNC )
.

( 1 male , CMNC )
.

Notes. We have examined specimens from the U.S.A. , Querétaro, and Tamaulipas ( Fig. 23 ) and can find no differences that would warrant separate species status. Dissections of male genitalia reveal no differences among the specimens. This is the species reported by
Jones et al. (2022)
.
